delivered: human-proposed and approved exact-domain research policies with a separate live-network flag
client/system-separated content-addressed evidence cache and local token index
offline approved evidence ingestion and cache-first/local-only degradation
isolated direct official-source HTTPS worker with DNS and IP pinning
HTTPS-only URL, redirect, public-address, MIME, encoding, byte, timeout, and rate controls
robots.txt enforcement, no cookies, no authentication, no JavaScript, and no compression
prompt-injection detection and permanent untrusted-content labeling
Landlock plus PID/IPC/UTS isolation preventing repository, broker, home, SSH, browser, and Docker reads
optional localhost-only SearXNG discovery interface and non-installed configuration template
source URL, retrieval time, raw/content hash, size, MIME, redirects, and isolation provenance
unresolved_risks: No live external fetch or SearXNG service was enabled or tested because separate network/service authorization was not granted
Domain owners, terms, robots behavior, cache permissions, and rates require project-specific human review
Public pages remain untrusted and may contain subtle prompt injection not detected by patterns
The design provides high practical availability and caching, not literally unlimited third-party search
DLP, secret capabilities, cloud adapters, and automated recovery integration remain unavailable
next_allowed_phase: phase-6-synthetic-secret-and-egress-DLP-only; selecting or using a real secret backend requires authorization
